title: 怪屋
summary:
Following the reading of Aristide Leonidis's will, Clementine defends Roger before leaving with him. Gasteyer, expressing disgust at the situation, organizes documents and offers Sophia financial advice and an advance payment, suggesting a future meeting at the Lincoln Hotel. Gasteyer notes that Roger is unfit for business, a sentiment Aristide had previously expressed. Sophia matter-of-factly states she has no intention of reviving the catering company. After Gasteyer departs, the atmosphere among the remaining family members turns cold.

Philip reacts with icy resentment, feeling humiliated and ignored by his father despite his lifelong dedication. Edith de Harvillan attempts to soothe him, suggesting Aristide simply favored the younger generation. Eustace, Aristide's grandson, suffers an emotional outburst, expressing intense hatred for his grandfather for leaving the entire inheritance to Sophia. He flees the room in a state of hysteria, prompting Margaret to chase after him while Edith watches snidely.

Sophia remains composed, though she expresses concern regarding Eustace's grudge and her father's potential hurt. She also mentions her refusal to use her new wealth to sponsor her mother's interest in Edith Thompson's play. The narrator observes that Sophia's character closely resembles Aristide's.

Later, while Sophia, Brenda, and the narrator are in the rock garden, Detective Taverna and Sheriff Lam arrive at the house. Brenda, visibly shaken by the ongoing investigation and the presence of reporters, questions their purpose. The narrator, aware of letters found in the water tank room, suspects the authorities have been to the prosecutor's office. Taverna approaches the group and informs Sophia that he has an arrest warrant for her, charging her with the poisoning of Aristide Leonidis on September 19th.
